coredata.dc:title: Novel SnO2 / Mesoporous Carbon Spheres Composite Anode for Li-ion Batteries
coredata.prism:copyright: Copyright © 2011 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.dc:creator: Di Lupo, Francesca
Gerbaldi, Claudio
Meligrana, Giuseppina
Bodoardo, Silvia
Penazzi, Nerino
coredata.dc:description: This work describes the preparation of a novel composite material made of nanosized SnO2 supported into ordered mesoporous carbon spheres (SnO2/MCS) through a two-step hydrothermal/impregnation method. The morphology, structure and composition of the samples have been characterized by means of atomic emission spectroscopy, powder X-ray diffraction, scanning electron microscopy and nitrogen-adsorption techniques. The experimental findings suggest that the supported tin oxide species, with loading amounts as high as 30 wt. %, are located and dispersed onto the mesopores of the carbon spheres. Cyclic voltammetry and galvanostatic discharge/charge cycling have been used to test the electrochemical behaviour as anodes in test cells using lithium foils as both the counter and the reference electrodes. The best electrode cycles with a specific capacity in the order of 500 mAh g–1 at C/10 current rate. It also performs well at high rate (1C rate), showing good capacity and excellent charge-discharge efficiency.
coredata.dcterms:subject: SnO2
mesoporous carbon
anode material
lithium ion battery

xocs:meta.xocs:articleinfo: rawtext articleinfo articletitlenorm authfirstinitialnorm authfirstsurnamenorm cid cids contenttype copyright crossmark dateloaded dateloadedtxt datesearch datesort dateupdated dco docsubtype doctype doi eid ewtransactionid hubeid indexeddate issfirst issn issnnorm issuelist itemstage itemtransactionid itemweight oauserlicense openaccess openarchive pg pgfirst pglast pii piinorm pubdateend pubdatestart pubdatetxt pubyr sortorder srctitle srctitlenorm srctype ssids alllist content oa subj tomb volfirst volissue volumelist webpdf webpdfpagecount yearnav affil articletitle auth authfirstini authfull authkeywords authlast primabst ref
xocs:meta.xocs:copyright-line: Copyright © 2011 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.

xocs:doc.xocs:rawtext: Novel SnO2 / Mesoporous Carbon Spheres Composite Anode for Li-ion BatteriesFrancesca Di Lupo, 1 Claudio Gerbaldi, 1 2 claudio.gerbaldi@iit.it claudio.gerbaldi@polito.it Giuseppina Meligrana, 1 Silvia Bodoardo, 1 3 Nerino Penazzi, 1 3 1 Department of Materials Science and Chemical Engineering, Politecnico di Torino; C.so Duca degli Abruzzi 24, 10129 Torino, Italy Department of Materials Science and Chemical Engineering Politecnico di Torino; C.so Duca degli Abruzzi 24 Torino 10129 Italy Department of Materials Science and Chemical Engineering, Politecnico di Torino; C.so Duca degli Abruzzi 24, 10129 Torino, Italy 2 Center for Space Human Robotics @Polito, Italian Institute of Technology; Corso Trento 21, 10129 Torino, Italy Center for Space Human Robotics @Polito Italian Institute of Technology Corso Trento 21 Torino 10129 Italy Center for Space Human Robotics @Polito, Italian Institute of Technology; Corso Trento 21, 10129 Torino, Italy 3 INSTM UdR Politecnico di Torino; C.so Duca degli Abruzzi 24, 10129 Torino, Italy INSTM UdR Politecnico di Torino; C.so Duca degli Abruzzi 24 Torino 10129 Italy INSTM UdR Politecnico di Torino; C.so Duca degli Abruzzi 24, 10129 Torino, Italy This work describes the preparation of a novel composite material made of nanosized SnO2 supported into ordered mesoporous carbon spheres (SnO supported into ordered mesoporous carbon spheres (SnO2 /MCS) through a two-step hydrothermal/impregnation method. The morphology, structure and composition of the samples have been characterized by means of atomic emission spectroscopy, powder X-ray diffraction, scanning electron microscopy and nitrogen-adsorption techniques. The experimental findings suggest that the supported tin oxide species, with loading amounts as high as 30 wt. %, are located and dispersed onto the mesopores of the carbon spheres. Cyclic voltammetry and galvanostatic discharge/charge cycling have been used to test the electrochemical behaviour as anodes in test cells using lithium foils as both the counter and the reference electrodes. The best electrode cycles with a specific capacity in the order of 500 mAh g /MCS) through a two-step hydrothermal/impregnation method. The morphology, structure and composition of the samples have been characterized by means of atomic emission spectroscopy, powder X-ray diffraction, scanning electron microscopy and nitrogen-adsorption techniques. The experimental findings suggest that the supported tin oxide species, with loading amounts as high as 30 wt. %, are located and dispersed onto the mesopores of the carbon spheres. Cyclic voltammetry and galvanostatic discharge/charge cycling have been used to test the electrochemical behaviour as anodes in test cells using lithium foils as both the counter and the reference electrodes. The best electrode cycles with a specific capacity in the order of 500 mAh gâ€“1 at C/10 current rate. It also performs well at high rate (1C rate), showing good capacity and excellent charge-discharge efficiency.Keywords SnO2 mesoporous carbon anode material lithium ion battery References 1 P.G. simple-head.ce:title: Novel SnO2 / Mesoporous Carbon Spheres Composite Anode for Li-ion Batteries

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Materials Science and Chemical Engineering, Politecnico di Torino; C.so Duca degli Abruzzi 24, 10129 Torino, Italy

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Department of Materials Science and Chemical Engineering
Politecnico di Torino; C.so Duca degli Abruzzi 24

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Department of Materials Science and Chemical Engineering, Politecnico di Torino; C.so Duca degli Abruzzi 24, 10129 Torino, Italy

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Center for Space Human Robotics @Polito, Italian Institute of Technology; Corso Trento 21, 10129 Torino, Italy
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Center for Space Human Robotics @Polito, Italian Institute of Technology; Corso Trento 21, 10129 Torino, Italy

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: INSTM UdR Politecnico di Torino; C.so Duca degli Abruzzi 24, 10129 Torino, Italy

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: INSTM UdR Politecnico di Torino; C.so Duca degli Abruzzi 24

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: INSTM UdR Politecnico di Torino; C.so Duca degli Abruzzi 24, 10129 Torino, Italy

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: This work describes the preparation of a novel composite material made of nanosized SnO2 supported into ordered mesoporous carbon spheres (SnO2/MCS) through a two-step hydrothermal/impregnation method. The morphology, structure and composition of the samples have been characterized by means of atomic emission spectroscopy, powder X-ray diffraction, scanning electron microscopy and nitrogen-adsorption techniques. The experimental findings suggest that the supported tin oxide species, with loading amounts as high as 30 wt. %, are located and dispersed onto the mesopores of the carbon spheres. Cyclic voltammetry and galvanostatic discharge/charge cycling have been used to test the electrochemical behaviour as anodes in test cells using lithium foils as both the counter and the reference electrodes. The best electrode cycles with a specific capacity in the order of 500 mAh g–1 at C/10 current rate. It also performs well at high rate (1C rate), showing good capacity and excellent charge-discharge efficiency.

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: 1. P.G. Bruce, B. Scrosati, and J.M. Tarason, Angew. Chem. Int. Ed., 47 (2008) 2930
